description: external Go cold-verify anchor for the Ledger-v2 chain — proves the independent Go verifier reproduces verify_chain verdict-for-verdict (P1-T04, SV-2 meta-rule M3)
rules: builds + runs the independent Go chain verifier (verifiers/go `chain`)
Go and Python verify_chain must agree on EVERY chain in the corpus — a divergence fails the gate
the corpus must exercise both poles (>=1 sound chain cold-verified, >=1 corrupt chain caught and named)
requires the `go` toolchain (skipped where absent; the codeqc CI job installs it / the compute image ships it)
usage: Set CHAIN_CORPUS (a corpus of named v2 chains, each {name, schema, entries, expect_run_id?, expect_plan_sha?, expect_ok?}). Output: anchor.json with {go_cold_verified, go_caught, match, diffs}.
limitation: Anchors the cryptographic prev-chain recompute; durability (torture/crashloop) arrives with P1-T02/P1-T09.
